pat_note_resolved_by_operator: 2026-04-25 (CORRECTED, supersedes 2026-04-23 entry): The 'update CLAUDE.md PAT' TODO was NOT a phantom. Project CLAUDE.md (md5 7caa81c513c47df50e47a5ee3ffc4903) is clean — that part of the 04-23 investigation was correct. But user-level CLAUDE.md (~/CLAUDE.md or ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, outside operator session visibility) did contain a Devilwench KEV-scoped PAT that the decider correctly identified each run. Operator confirmed Devilwench PAT was already expired/invalid and removed it from user-level CLAUDE.md on 2026-04-25. The earlier HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-PHANTOM-001 has been superseded by HNOTE-DECIDER-CLAUDE-MD-PAT-CORRECTED-002 with the corrected story. Decider agents: do not re-flag this; source is removed.
notes_this_run: Mode 2 (Human Notes) processed: HNOTE-OPERATOR-EXP-254-9-STATION-COVERAGE-001 consumed by writing EXP-260. New addendum to §4.2.7 (the eclipse-geometry trilemma) gives the quantitative station-by-station kill: of the dome's 9 published eclipse stations, 0 lie within the 42-km dome-geometry penumbra radius and 0 within the 85-km band (closest Ebro at 444 km / 10.6x R_p; furthest Canary Islands at 4,084 km / 97x R_p). Constant-r orbital reading: max 1 station can be in the band at all (closest pair Coimbra-San Pablo dr=150 km > 85-km band). Inflated R_p needed for full coverage = 4,084 km, equivalent to Moon altitude ~246,000 km (97x dome's 2,534). Pre-empts time-distribution rebuttal: NN-tour 16,439 km > 15,660 km max arc at 87 km/min. New SVG eclipse-stations-vs-penumbra-band.svg embedded in EXP-260 new_files. Blocked on EXP-254 integration. Hnote pending: 0.

PHASE 3 — SINGLETON SUBSTANTIVE (ATT-2026-04-26T17:30:06Z, Overview-hero, EXP-261 integration):
  Verified the decider's EXP-261 integration in build-scripts/generate-html.js (workspace; NOT yet pushed to git, awaiting workspace-sync rescue):

EXP-261's invariant_v2 should drop this assertion or re-state as expected=2.

USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ("Think about the other side too"): Considered the strongest defender objection to OBE — that an old item might still flag a real gap that the curmudgeon review didn't catch. Mitigation per spec: every OBE has explicit one-line rationale; high-priority items excluded; integrity agent can spot-check OBE rates. The 10 OBE'd items here all reference closed downstream work (renumbered sections, integrated EXPs with later c3/c4/c5/c10/c15 curmudgeon supersession). Risk of dismissing legitimate-but-stale concerns is real but bounded by the explicit traceability of the rationale.

OBSERVATION (worth flagging to tinker/integrity): The prior analyst run @ 15:04:23 noted resolution of ATT-2026-04-12T17:45:35.302Z but the workspace inbox showed it as still pending. Most likely cause: decider's 17:30:06Z FUSE-only push (which added ATT-2026-04-26T17:30:06Z) used a stale base and clobbered the analyst's resolution. This is a known multi-writer race on attention-inbox.json. The current run re-resolves the same item via OBE C4 supersession criterion, so the immediate state is consistent — but the pattern suggests the universal-pusher rescue isn't fast enough between consecutive analyst+decider runs. Suggested follow-up for tinker: detect "resolved → re-pending" transitions in attention-inbox history.
